faq.question: What accuracy can I expect from PDF data extraction tools?
faq.answer: Accuracy varies significantly by tool and document type. Most tools achieve 85-95% accuracy on clear, typed documents like invoices. Scanned documents typically see 70-90% accuracy depending on image quality and OCR capabilities.

faq.question: How much do PDF data extraction tools typically cost?
faq.answer: Pricing ranges from free tiers with limited pages to enterprise solutions costing $500+ monthly. Pay-per-page models range from $0.10-$1.00 per page, while subscription plans typically start around $29-99 monthly for small businesses.

faq.question: Which document types work best with automated extraction?
faq.answer: Structured documents like invoices, bank statements, purchase orders, and receipts typically have the highest accuracy rates. Complex reports with nested tables or heavily formatted layouts may require more manual review.

faq.question: Can these tools handle scanned PDFs and images?
faq.answer: Most modern tools include OCR capabilities for scanned documents and images. However, accuracy drops compared to digital PDFs, especially with poor scan quality, skewed images, or faded text.

trustSignals: AI-powered extraction with custom field selection
